title: 刚刚，Claude Code大升级！卡帕西：LLM第三次变革 – 量子位

evidence: 官方将其定位为Claude Code的一次进化：更主动、更擅长团队协作。Anthropic透露，目前公司约65%的产品代码已经由Claude Tag参与完成。卡帕西表示：这是LLM用户界面的第三次重大变革。

title: 周鸿祎ISC宣布打造中国版Mythos，360联手信创巨头发起“磐石之盾”安全协作计划 – 量子位

周鸿祎认为Mythos能自主发现漏洞、分析漏洞、构造攻击武器，形成战略威慑
summary: 在ISC.AI 2026上，360集团创始人周鸿祎发布了AI安全核心能力，包括漏洞自动化挖掘智能体“图龙锋”和网络安全自动化防御系统“仪天阵”。同时，360联合飞腾、麒麟等发起“磐石之盾”安全协作计划。周鸿祎认为Anthropic的Mythos模型改变了网络攻防平衡。
evidence: 周鸿祎正式发布360 AI安全“倚天屠龙”两大核心能力：漏洞自动化挖掘智能体“图龙锋”和网络安全自动化防御系统“仪天阵”。360联合飞腾、麒麟等共同发起“磐石之盾”安全协作计划。周鸿祎认为Mythos能自主发现漏洞、分析漏洞甚至构造网络攻击“武器”。

evidence: 2026年上半年（截至6月12日），国内具身智能赛道共融资约438亿元。超过一半的钱涌入了“大脑派”公司。融资速度仿佛坐上了火箭，「平均一个月融资一轮」甚至成了行业常态。

title: 陶哲轩12年前的预言，现在AI帮他兑现了 – 量子位

AI在数学领域加速，OpenAI和DeepMind取得进展
陶哲轩推进Lean形式化证明和Equational Theories项目
summary: 陶哲轩12年前关于形式化语言撰写论文的预言正在被AI实现。过去一年，AI在数学领域加速，OpenAI和DeepMind取得突破。陶哲轩本人也通过Lean形式化证明和Equational Theories项目推动AI与人类协作。
evidence: 12年前，陶哲轩在首届数学突破奖的台上抛出的一句预言：将来有一天，我们或许不再用LaTeX撰写论文，而是使用计算机能理解的形式化语言。过去这一年，AI在数学领域突然开始疯狂提速。从OpenAI解决开放问题，到DeepMind批量攻克数学猜想。

title: AI转型最大的门槛，不是技术，是人 – 量子位
entities: 清华大学全球产业研究院
key_facts: 浪潮信息董事长彭震认为AI转型最大门槛是组织、文化、流程
麦肯锡调研显示88%企业至少一个场景常态化使用AI
彭震提出Humagent（Human+Agent）概念
summary: 浪潮信息董事长彭震在AIEC2026上指出，AI转型的最大门槛不是技术，而是组织、文化和流程。麦肯锡调研显示88%企业已常态化使用AI，但仅三分之一计划规模化落地。彭震提出Humagent概念，描述未来企业基本构成单元。
evidence: 浪潮信息董事长彭震在人工智能+生态大会（AIEC2026）上抛出判断：AI转型最大的门槛，不是技术，是人。麦肯锡的调研显示，已经有88%的企业，内部至少有一个场景实现了AI的常态化使用。彭震创造新词Humagent，Human+Agent。

evidence: DeepWay深向 的智能新能源重卡，最近接连实现规模化交付。 鸭嘴兽、马士基、安能、申通 ——这些货运物流行业的老玩家，正在用真金白银为智能驾驶技术买单。

title: 抖音发布3D内容创作工具“造世界”

该工具集成多Agents，可生成可发布、可游玩的虚拟世界模型
服务于抖音3D UGC场景“多闪仔仔世界”创作
summary: 抖音正加速布局3D UGC互动内容赛道。6月24日，抖音在火山引擎FORCE大会上首次亮相“造世界”，这是一款集成了多Agents的3D世界创作工具。创作者可以通过多轮对话、素材搭建和场景编辑，生成可发布、可游玩的虚拟世界模型。据了解，“造世界”主要服务于抖音3D UGC场景“多闪仔仔世界”创作，作品创作完成后，可进入抖音和多闪的社交与推荐分发场景。目前，创作者可在“抖音虚拟创作平台”官网同步申请内测。
evidence: 抖音在火山引擎FORCE大会上首次亮相“造世界”，这是一款集成了多Agents的3D世界创作工具。

title: 圆桌对话：诶? AI! AI这个月、AI这一年、AI这五年｜36氪WAVES2026新浪潮
key_facts: 圆桌讨论AI投资趋势
嘉宾认为AI融资节奏加快
Anthropic模型因安全问题限制非美国公民使用
中国大模型公司纷纷开源
summary: “2026年，创投圈的浪潮再次翻涌：AI从技术概念走进产业深水区，硬科技创业从“小众赛道” 变成“主流共识”，年轻的创业者们正在用代码和双手，重新定义中国创新的未来坐标。 每一年，由36氪 · 暗涌主办的WAVES大会，都是中国创投圈的年度风向标。 今年的 WAVES 2026以“今年盛夏”为主题，落地广州番禺良仓新造创意园，在两天的时间里，我们汇聚了顶级投资人、产业领袖、新锐创业者，用14场深度圆桌、数十场独立演讲，拆解 AI、硬科技、出海、医疗等核心赛道的底层逻辑，见证那些“少数人” 的坚持，如何汇聚成改变行业的浪潮。”
evidence: 最近半年最让我震撼的，是AI相关各个细分领域的融资节奏，跟以前完全不一样，说实话非常震惊。

title: InSight: Self-Guided Skill Acquisition via Steerable VLAs
key_facts: InSight框架使VLA模型在原始动作层面可操控。
自动分割演示为标记原语，实现VLA原语可操控性。
VLM引导的数据飞轮自动识别缺失原语并尝试演示。
在模拟和真实任务中评估，无需人类演示目标技能。
summary: InSight框架通过可操控的VLA模型实现机器人自主技能获取，无需人类演示即可学习新技能并组合执行长时任务。
evidence: InSight框架解锁自主技能获取，通过VLM引导的数据飞轮，无需人类演示目标技能。

title: New Bounds for the Last Iterate of the Stochastic subGradient Method
key_facts: proves optimization error of order 1/√n for last iterate under i.i.d. noise
shows without i.i.d. assumption error can be (log n)/√n
summary: We study the last iterate of the stochastic subgradient method for one-dimensional convex Lipschitz objectives. For a fixed horizon $n$, we consider the standard fixed stepsizes $η=Θ(1/\sqrt n)$. We prove that, for such stepsize policies, under additive i.i.d. subgradient noise with uniformly bounded variance, the last iterate features an optimization error of order $1/\sqrt n$, thereby removing the extra $(\log n)$ factor present in existing generic bounds. On the other hand, we show that without the i.i.d. assumption, the optimization error can be of order $(\log n)/\sqrt n$. Thus, under the uniformly bounded variance assumption alone, the last iterate of SsGM is suboptimal even in dimension one, resolving negatively an open problem posed in Koren and Segal, COLT, 2020.
evidence: proves optimization error of order 1/√n for last iterate under i.i.d. noise; shows without i.i.d. assumption error can be (log n)/√n

title: FLUX3D: High-Fidelity 3D Gaussian Generation with Diffusion-Aligned Sparse Representation
key_facts: proposes FLUX3D framework for image-to-3DGS generation
introduces Diffusion-Aligned Structured Latents (DA-SLAT) and Sparse-structure Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (SMDiT)
outperforms SOTA methods in appearance fidelity
summary: Sparse voxel representation has emerged as a scalable foundation for image-to-3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) generation, yet current methods struggle to preserve high-frequency visual details of input images due to two structural bottlenecks. First, they adopt discriminative 2D features optimized for semantic abstraction to construct sparse voxel latents, which suppress reconstructive cues and induce a representation bottleneck. Second, in the generation stage, standard diffusion transformers lack effective mechanisms to align dense 2D image tokens with sparse 3D voxel latents, resulting in a cross-modal correspondence bottleneck. To address these issues, we propose FLUX3D, a scalable image-to-3DGS framework that boosts both representation learning and cross-modal alignment during generation. We first revisit 2D feature selection for sparse-voxel-based 3D representation learning, propose Diffusion-Aligned Structured Latents (DA-SLAT) and couple it with a decoder-only architecture to improve 3DGS reconstruction fidelity. We also design a sparse-structure-aware diffusion framework, which integrates the Sparse-structure Multimodal Diffusion Transformer (SMDiT) and Modal-Aware Rotary Positional Embedding (MARoPE) to achieve geometry-agnostic 2D-3D alignment. Extensive benchmark experiments demonstrate that FLUX3D yields substantial improvements in appearance fidelity and significantly outperforms all state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods in generating high-quality 3DGS assets.
evidence: proposes FLUX3D framework; introduces DA-SLAT and SMDiT; outperforms SOTA methods

title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
key_facts: releases fully open data curation pipeline for training agentic models
fine-tunes Qwen3-32B on 100K examples achieving 44.8% average accuracy across seven benchmarks
improves 3.9 percentage points over Nemotron-Terminal-32B
summary: Agentic language models dramatically expand the applications of AI yet little is publicly known about how to curate training data for broadly capable agents. Existing open efforts such as SWE-Smith, SERA, and Nemotron-Terminal typically target a single benchmark, leaving open the question of how to train models that generalize across diverse agentic tasks. The OpenThoughts-Agent (OT-Agent) project addresses this gap with a fully open data curation pipeline for training agentic models. We conduct more than 100 controlled ablation experiments to systematically investigate each stage of the pipeline, yielding insights on the importance of task sources and diversity. We then assemble a training set of 100K examples from our pipeline and fine-tune Qwen3-32B on this dataset, which yields an average accuracy of 44.8% across seven agentic benchmarks and a 3.9 percentage point improvement over the strongest existing open data agentic model (Nemotron-Terminal-32B, 40.9%). Moreover, our training data exhibits strong scaling properties, outperforming alternative open datasets at every training set size in compute-controlled comparisons. We publicly release our training sets, data pipeline, experimental data, and models at openthoughts.ai to support future open research on agentic model training.
evidence: releases fully open data curation pipeline; fine-tunes Qwen3-32B achieving 44.8% average accuracy; improves 3.9 percentage points over Nemotron-Terminal-32B

title: It's Complicated: On the Design and Evaluation of AI-Powered AAC Interfaces
key_facts: explores six AAC problem spaces
suggests more robust evaluation methods considering intersectional nuances
summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) can enhance what people who use augmentative and alternative communication (AAC) are able to do with their systems. However, evaluating AI-powered AAC interfaces can be difficult. People are intersectional beings and current evaluation metrics can struggle to capture the multifaceted and nuanced desires people may have for their AAC. We explore the complicated nature of six AAC problem spaces, explore how AI might be used in these spaces, and suggest more robust methods of evaluation that take the intersectional nuances of people into account. We also discuss broader issues that arise across these problem spaces and how they could be addressed using our proposed evaluation methods.
evidence: explores six AAC problem spaces; suggests more robust evaluation methods

title: Real vs. Complex Spectral Bases for Neural Operators: The Role of Green's Function Alignment
key_facts: introduces Hartley Neural Operator (HNO) as real-valued mirror of FNO
shows elliptic operators favor HNO, time-dependent operators favor FNO
summary: Fourier Neural Operators (FNO) learn solution operators of partial differential equations by parameterizing global convolutions in the complex Fourier domain. For real-valued PDE solutions, the complex FFT carries representational redundancy through conjugate symmetry. We introduce the Hartley Neural Operator (HNO), the exact real-valued mirror of FNO: it replaces the FFT with the purely real Discrete Hartley Transform and learns a single real multiplier per retained spectral mode, with no complex arithmetic. Because the real Hartley spectrum is not halved by conjugate symmetry, HNO retains twice as many frequency corners as FNO but one real weight where FNO carries a complex pair, so the two operators are iso-parametric at equal width and differ only in spectral basis. Our central thesis is that the best basis is a property of the operator. Self-adjoint elliptic operators (Poisson, biharmonic) have real, symmetric Green's functions that the real Hartley multiplier diagonalizes exactly, and HNO is favored there. Time-dependent operators carry phase, from oscillation in the wave equation to transport in advection, Burgers, and Navier-Stokes, which a real diagonal multiplier cannot represent, so FNO is favored there, and increasingly so with the operator's phase content, leaving the phaseless heat equation as the borderline case. Training both operators identically and benchmarking across PDE classes, initial-condition families, and boundary conditions, we find an elliptic-versus-time-dependent split that is monotone in operator phase content and matches the Green's-function theory we develop. Rather than a universal winner, our findings give a predictive rule: match the spectral basis to the symmetry of the solution operator.
evidence: introduces Hartley Neural Operator; shows elliptic operators favor HNO, time-dependent operators favor FNO
